summary:
Alpha-thalassemias are characterized primarily due to reduced synthesis of α-globin chains compared to β-chains; this leads to accumulation of abnormal HbF and subsequent cellular damage from excess non-α-containing globin dimers. The most prevalent form does not completely lack any α-chains but rather has varying degrees of deficiency affecting red blood cell function significantly more than just absence as suggested for B.
 To finalize decision:

Option A is correct because it accurately describes the primary pathology involving insufficient α-globin subunit production which drives disease manifestations seen clinically across different types of alpha-thalassemia disorders. Option B incorrectly implies complete loss of all four copies of genes encoding these crucial proteins when such cases represent only one extreme end among many possible phenotypes ranging widely The most prevalent form does not completely lack any α-chains but rather has varying degrees of deficiency affecting red blood cell function significantly more than just absence as suggested for B.
 To finalize decision:
